entities.title: Entities
entities.operationalOverdue: You have overdue payments of {amount}. This may affect benefit or voting rights depending on the path rules.
entities.operationalOverdueAndPendingVotes: You have overdue payments of {amount} and {count} open decision awaiting your vote; open the entity to review money and governance together.
entities.operationalPendingVoteHint: You have {count} open decision awaiting your vote in this entity.
entities.operationalConditional: You have a conditional subscription awaiting activation; rights are not complete until the condition is approved.
entities.operationalSupporterOnly: You are supporter-only in this entity; you contribute without direct benefit rights.
entities.operationalActive: Your rights are active across {count} path; open the entity to see related wallets and decisions.
entities.operationalNoSubscription: You do not have operational subscriptions in this entity yet.
entities.operationalPendingReview: This entity is under platform review; some actions may remain paused until review is complete.
entities.operationalSuspended: This entity is currently suspended; access is limited and new financial actions are blocked.
entities.operationalReadOnly: This entity is read-only; you can review information without creating new financial actions.
entities.operationalCampaignClosed: The campaign has ended and is now review-only; donations, disbursements, and logs remain visible without new actions.
entities.relationshipWhyText: You can see this page because you have a {role} membership in this entity.
entities.relationshipMapTitle: My subscription map in this entity
entities.relationshipMapHint: Each row links your membership to a wallet, path, and rights state.
entities.relationshipMapEmpty: Your membership exists, but it is not yet linked to a clear operational wallet, path, and subscription.
entities.relationshipRightsActive: This subscription activates your obligations and grants voting or benefit rights according to this path's rules.
entities.relationshipRightsConditional: You are participating with a condition; the obligation is visible, but rights are not complete until the condition is approved.
entities.relationshipRightsInterested: You have only expressed interest; no operational rights or obligations start until the subscription becomes active or conditional.
entities.relationshipRightsSuspended: This subscription is suspended; benefit or voting rights may stop until the suspension reason is resolved.
entities.relationshipRightsExited: This subscription is exited and grants no current rights or obligations.
entities.relationshipRightsSupporterOnly: You are supporter-only in this path; you contribute to the balance without direct benefit or disbursement request rights.
entities.relationshipOverdue: You have overdue payments of {amount}. Benefit or voting eligibility may be affected depending on path rules.
entities.relationshipConditional: You have a conditional subscription; the obligation is visible, but rights are not complete until the condition is activated.
entities.relationshipSupporterOnly: You are supporter-only in this entity; your contribution does not grant direct benefit rights.
entities.relationshipActive: You are active in {count} path. The wallets and decisions linked to these paths define your rights and obligations.
entities.relationshipNoSubscription: Your entity membership is active, but no operational subscription is linked to a wallet and path yet.
entities.pendingReviewTitle: This entity has not moved to full operation yet
entities.pendingReviewBody: You can prepare data and review memberships and wallets, but do not treat this entity as a complete operating experience until platform review is done and the status becomes active.
entities.pendingReviewReasonFallback: The platform has not provided a detailed reason yet; check the review center or access log.
entities.pendingReviewBankMissing: Missing; add bank and account details before financial operation
entities.pendingReviewMembersMissing: Not enough memberships to test operation
entities.setupChecklistBody: The fund exists now. These steps unlock services gradually: basic details, bank details, wallet, governance path, then member invitations.
entities.setupChecklistBasicMissing: Add a short description so members understand why the fund exists.
entities.setupChecklistBankDone: Bank details are preliminarily complete at {bank}.
entities.setupChecklistBankMissing: Add bank name and account number before serious financial operation.
entities.setupChecklistWalletMissing: Create at least one wallet to define where money is collected.
entities.setupChecklistPathNeedsWallet: Start with a wallet, then connect it to a governance path.
entities.setupChecklistPathMissing: Add a path that defines voting, committee, or decision method.
entities.setupChecklistMembersDone: {count} memberships are visible inside the fund.
entities.setupChecklistMembersMissing: Invite at least one member after the purpose and wallet are prepared.
entities.campaignClosedTitle: The campaign has ended and moved to read-only mode
entities.campaignClosedBody: You can review donations, executed disbursements, decisions, and related appeals. New wallets, disbursement requests, and financial actions are paused so campaign funds do not change after closure.
entities.campaignClosedFootnote: If an appeal or privacy document appears after closure, open a dispute or review the audit timeline instead of creating a new disbursement.
entities.noWalletsTitle: No wallets have been created for this entity yet
entities.noWalletsBody: Membership alone is not enough for operation. Create a wallet first, then connect it to a governance path and subscriptions so members know where they pay and what rights they have.
entities.walletSectionHint: Each wallet represents an independent financial purpose with its own policies and paths.
entities.walletDescriptionPlaceholder: Briefly describe what this wallet funds and who benefits from it.
entities.relationDescMembershipOverlap: Members overlap between the two entities; the goal is to show the overlap without mixing obligations.
entities.relationDescFinancialSupport: One entity financially supports another without automatically transferring membership or decision rights.
entities.relationDescSharedWallet: A shared wallet or purpose between two entities; ownership split and rights must be clear before disbursement.
entities.relationDescManagementDelegation: One entity manages a defined scope on behalf of another under the relationship terms.
entities.relationDescMerger: Preparatory relationship for merging an entity or wallet into another entity.
entities.relationDescContributionNoVote: The supporter funds the purpose only and does not vote on entity or wallet decisions.
entities.relationDescContributionWithOversight: The supporter has reporting and oversight rights without turning that oversight into direct financial voting power.
entities.relationDescReportSharing: Reports or indicators are shared only, without money movement or voting rights.
entities.relationDescDefault: Operational relationship between two entities; review its terms before approval.
entities.compatNoActive: No active paths or subscriptions to analyze.
entities.transferHint: Change a member's operational path from the membership management area without leaving the membership context.
entities.transferNeedsPaths: At least two active paths are required to transfer a member.
entities.transferNoSub: No active subscription found in the source path
entities.memberOpsRightsActive: Has voting and benefit rights in {count} active path according to each path's rules.
entities.memberOpsRightsConditional: Conditional participant; rights and obligations should not be treated as fully active until the condition is reviewed.
entities.memberOpsRightsSupporterOnly: Supporter only; contributes financially without benefit or voting rights in this path.
entities.memberOpsRightsSuspended: Subscriptions are suspended; do not count this member for voting or benefits until reactivated.
entities.memberOpsRightsInactive: Inactive membership; no operational rights should appear inside the entity right now.
entities.memberOpsRightsNoSubscription: No clear subscription relationship with a governance path; link this membership to a wallet and path before calculating rights.
entities.typeFamilyDesc: Private family fund for individuals and relatives
entities.typeBuildingDesc: Fund for a building or residential complex
entities.typeNeighborhoodDesc: Fund for a neighborhood or geographic community
entities.fundFlowTitle: What do you want to create?
entities.fundFlowFundBody: A durable space that can start empty or from a template, then expand with wallets and paths.
entities.fundFlowCampaignBody: A time-limited initiative attached to an existing fund with its own lifecycle.
entities.campaignNeedsParent: A campaign needs an existing fund before it can be created.
entities.campaignEndDateInvalid: Choose a future campaign end date.
entities.campaignPlanSubtitle: A campaign is temporary and needs its financial and operating context separated from the durable fund.
entities.campaignPlanDurationOpen: No end date; it will need manual closure later
entities.campaignPlanFinanceParentBankReady: Financial custody starts from {bank}, then a campaign wallet is prepared
entities.campaignPlanFinanceParentBankMissing: Parent fund bank details are incomplete
entities.campaignPlanGovernanceDetail: Disbursement and review happen through the campaign path after setup
entities.campaignPlanAfterCreateBody: Open the campaign page and create a dedicated wallet or donation path before receiving payments or executing disbursements.
entities.campaignPlanOutcome: Creating a campaign does not move balances or create automatic disbursement; it opens a temporary space under the fund.
entities.wizardDescPlaceholder: Brief description of the fund's purpose and goals…
entities.wizardGovHint: Can be adjusted later for each wallet independently.
entities.govPublicVoteDesc: All members vote on every decision
entities.govIndividualDesc: Each member acts independently within a defined limit
entities.wizardTemplateHint: Templates automatically create default wallets and paths.
entities.noTemplateDesc: Start from scratch and add wallets manually
entities.fundStartModeHint: Choose an operating starting point. Wallets, paths, and rules can be adjusted later inside the fund.
entities.fundStartEmptyBody: Creates only the fund, then you complete the wallet and path when the operating model is clearer.
entities.fundStartEmptyOutcome: Best when you are not sure about the details yet.
entities.fundTemplateCustomBody: A general start for basic contributions and shared expenses that can grow later.
entities.fundTemplateCustomOutcome: Creates a starter wallet and a basic decision path.
entities.fundTemplateMutualAidBody: Suitable for medical help and cases that need documents and higher privacy.
entities.fundTemplateMutualAidOutcome: Creates a mutual-aid wallet and a committee review path.
entities.fundTemplateSharedServicesBody: Suitable for maintenance, security, elevators, and services shared by everyone.
entities.fundTemplateSharedServicesOutcome: Creates a services wallet and a services committee path.
entities.fundTemplateSupportBody: Suitable for collecting supporter contributions without automatic benefit rights for payers.
entities.fundTemplateSupportOutcome: Creates a support wallet and a supporter path.
entities.fundTemplateDisclosure: These are starting choices only; they do not prevent adding more wallets, paths, or rules later.
entities.wizardMultiPathTitle: Will you divide members into groups with different obligations?
entities.wizardMultiPathHint: For example: some members pay 200 SAR/month and others pay 500 — this requires multiple paths. If everyone has the same obligation, choose a single path.
entities.wizardMultiPathHint2: You can change this later from entity settings
entities.multiPathNote: For large groups with multiple tiers
entities.successDesc: You can now add wallets, members, and governance paths.
entities.legalItem1: This entity is <bold>not a registered legal entity</bold> — the platform is an organizational tool only and bears no legal responsibility for financial operations.
entities.legalItem2: As a founder, you are responsible for the <bold>integrity of financial operations</bold> and ensuring members comply with agreed-upon rules.
entities.legalItem3: Entered data will be visible to members <bold>according to the transparency policy</bold> you define for each wallet.
entities.legalItem4: In case of disputes, the transaction log stored in the system will be referenced — ensure the accuracy of every financial entry.
entities.legalAccept: I acknowledge the above and accept responsibility for managing this entity
entities.legalAcceptMissing: You must accept the liability acknowledgment first
entities.unverifiedDesc: To create an entity, you must first verify your account by confirming your phone number or email.
entities.preGateTitle: Before you start — the responsibility of creating a fund
entities.preGateSubtitle: Creating an entity means establishing a social financial fund governed by binding bylaws. Read the following before continuing:
entities.preGateItem1Title: The fund belongs to its members
entities.preGateItem1Body: Financial and membership data is visible only to entity members — it is not public.
entities.preGateItem2Title: You are accountable to your members
entities.preGateItem2Body: As the founder, you are the first point of reference for any dispute or appeal within the entity.
entities.preGateItem3Title: The platform cannot modify your financial records
entities.preGateItem3Body: The platform team has no permission to modify any financial record within your entity — all access is logged and visible to you.
entities.preGateItem4Body: You can export your entity's data at any time — even if the account is suspended.
entities.setupMapSubtitle: Before the first payment or disbursement, connect the entity to a wallet, path, and clear subscription so every member understands their rights and obligations.
entities.setupMapOutcome: Do not start disbursements before a governance path and clear decision exist; do not request payment before members can see their subscription and why the amount is due.
entities.nextStep1Desc: Share the invitation link with those you want to join
entities.nextStep2Desc: Define where funds are collected and how they are distributed
entities.nextStep2DescTemplate: Make sure every wallet has a clear purpose and benefit type before inviting members to pay.
entities.nextStep3Desc: Payment terms, caps, and financial rules
entities.nextStep4Desc: Accepting a member is not enough; confirm their role, subscription, and the path that controls their rights.
entities.nextStep5Desc: Before real operation, open finance and confirm dues and balances appear under the correct entity, wallet, and path.

finance.title: Finance & Accounts
finance.duesEmpty: No due payments at this time
finance.duesHint: You can select any due payment and upload a proof of payment
finance.duesEmptyMsg: No due payments in this entity.
finance.gatewaySessionCreated: Payment session created via {provider}. Session ID: {id}
finance.gatewayError: Failed to connect to payment gateway — please try again
finance.payFormHint: This form sends the payment proof for review, then it is recorded in the financial ledger.
finance.paymentImpactTitle: Payment proof impact preview before submission
finance.impactNoDecisionPayment: No disbursement decision is required; it still requires due matching and review approval.
finance.impactNoDecisionReview: Operational financial approval, not a governance disbursement decision.
finance.dueImpactOutcome: Paying or uploading proof does not change the balance immediately; it changes only after confirmation or a trusted webhook.
finance.paymentImpactOutcome: The proof will be sent for review. It will not count as paid or update the balance until approved.
finance.reviewImpactOutcome: When approved, the payment is recorded in the financial ledger and settles the due payment for this path only.
finance.cancelImpactOutcome: Cancelling the proof stops the review and does not change the path balance or mark the due as confirmed.
finance.paySubmitSuccess: ✓ Payment proof uploaded and sent for review
finance.myRecordsHint: Track the status of every payment proof in this entity
finance.reviewsHint: This list is for the admin, founder, or treasurer
finance.reviewsEmpty: No payment requests in this entity.
finance.approveConfirm: Do you want to approve this payment and record it in the financial ledger?
finance.cancelConfirm: Do you want to cancel the payment proof before review?
finance.chooseEntity: Select an entity to view its financial summary.
finance.prompt: Select an entity and wallet to view financial data
finance.rejectNotesPlaceholder: Enter the rejection reason clearly so the member can understand…
finance.paymentRule: Your payment proof is sent to the treasurer for review and confirmation. Once confirmed, it counts toward the entity records.
finance.balanceExplanationText: Balances here come from the financial ledger. A payment proof does not increase the balance until approved, and a disbursement does not reduce it unless it is tied to a valid governance decision and a specific path.
finance.balanceExplanationNoLatest: No confirmed payment approval is visible in this scope yet.
finance.balanceExplanationReviewerId: Approved by internal user reference {id}. The full name and action sequence appear in the audit log.
finance.balanceExplanationReviewerAudit: The approver name and change sequence are recorded in the audit log when the payment is approved.
finance.balanceExplanationDues: Current overdue amount is {overdue}. Rejected payment records in this scope: {rejected}.
finance.balanceExplanationAudit: For detailed review: open the Audit Timeline to see who did what and when, linked to the payment or disbursement ledger record.

wallets.noPathsTitle: This wallet has no governance path yet
wallets.noPathsBody: The balance, voting, and benefit rights cannot be interpreted before a path exists. Return to the entity context to complete wallets and paths before requesting any payment.
wallets.choosePathForTx: Select a path to view its transactions
wallets.approveConfirm: Do you approve linking this wallet?
wallets.rejectConfirm: Do you want to reject this wallet link?
wallets.relRejectedBy: Rejected by the target wallet admin
wallets.walletRelDescShared: This is a shared wallet relationship; it may grant voting and oversight depending on contribution split and terms.
wallets.walletRelDescSupport: This is financial support; the contribution helps the target wallet but does not automatically grant voting rights.
wallets.walletRelDescReportOnly: This is oversight/reporting only; there is no financial contribution and no voting right.
wallets.walletRelDescDefault: Operational relationship between wallets; review rights before approval.
wallets.walletRelFormSupportHint: Financial support contributes to the balance or purpose, but it does not grant voting rights. Keep oversight enabled when the supporter needs reports.
wallets.walletRelFormSharedHint: A shared wallet may grant voting and oversight depending on the agreement; confirm contribution percentage before sending.
wallets.walletRelFormReportOnlyHint: Reports only carries no contribution and no voting; use it for oversight and transparency.
wallets.balanceTransferHint: This creates a governed review request and does not move balance directly.
wallets.balanceTransferReasonPlaceholder: Example: cover an obligation in another path
wallets.balanceTransferRequestCreated: ✓ Transfer request created and sent for review
wallets.balanceTransferFieldsRequired: Please fill all transfer request fields
wallets.balanceTransferChoosePathForRequests: Choose a path to view transfer requests
wallets.balanceTransferNoRequests: No transfer requests for this path.
wallets.approveConfirmDesc: You are about to approve this wallet link. This can be undone later.
wallets.rejectConfirmDesc: The link request will be rejected immediately.
wallets.walletRelationshipText: This wallet holds the balance and separates operations through {count} governance path; each path has independent decisions, rights, and obligations.
wallets.walletRelationshipNoPaths: No active paths yet; rights and obligations cannot be interpreted before a path exists.
wallets.walletRelationshipMultiPath: Note: do not mix balances or decisions across paths. Approval in one path does not authorize another path.
wallets.pathMatrixIsolation: This balance and its decisions belong only to this path; permissions do not automatically carry to another path.
wallets.pathImpactCommittee: Disbursement decisions go through a defined committee; voting and benefit eligibility follow this path's membership.
wallets.pathImpactPublicVote: Decisions go to a public vote inside the path; a member's vote does not apply outside this path.
wallets.pathImpactIndividual: Individual spending is allowed up to a cap; anything beyond it needs additional governance or a decision.
wallets.pathImpactDonation: Designed for support and donations; it may not grant automatic benefit rights to contributors.
wallets.pathImpactEmergency: Allows rapid emergency response with later review so oversight is not lost.
wallets.pathImpactDefault: An independent path with its own rules, rights, and obligations inside this wallet.
wallets.sharedBenefitTitle: Shared benefit that cannot be fully separated
wallets.sharedBenefitText: This wallet represents a service that usually benefits everyone, such as a guard or elevator. Showing only the balance is not enough; track who supports it, who is overdue, and the shortfall before deciding.
wallets.sharedBenefitFreeRiderWarning: There is overdue or inactive support. The service may still benefit everyone, so treat this as shared coverage and deficit, not an individual medical wallet.
wallets.sharedBenefitHealthy: No clear deficit signal right now; keep reviewing dues because a shared benefit can be affected quickly by delayed payment.

rules.title: Rules
rules.chooseEntityPrompt: Select an entity, wallet, and path to manage rules.
rules.targetNote: The rule will be created at level:
rules.invalidJson: Rule data must be a valid JSON object.
rules.invalidJsonSyntax: Invalid JSON syntax in rule data.
rules.duplicateRule: ⚠ A matching rule already exists (same type and JSON data) at this level.
rules.templatesHint: Templates compatible with the current application level ({targetType}) are shown

designer.requiresAppealsEnabled: Requires appeals mechanism to be enabled for the entity
designer.invalidJsonSwitch: Invalid JSON. Please fix it before switching to the visual designer.
designer.visualModeNotSupported: Visual designer is not supported for type {type}

rules.choosePolicyEntityPrompt: Choose an entity to view the policy designer
rules.rulesContextSummary: Active rules here are automatically applied to every disbursement request and decision passing through this path. Conflicts between rules are resolved by priority.

disbursements.title: Record Disbursement
disbursements.choosePrompt: Select an entity, wallet, and path to record a disbursement.
disbursements.noApprovedDecisions: No approved disbursement decisions for this path. A DISBURSE_FUNDS decision must be created and closed with approval first.
disbursements.governanceRule: Disbursement is restricted to an approved Disburse-Funds decision linked to an active spending item in the path. This condition cannot be bypassed.
disbursements.confirmDescription: This will debit path {source} into spending item {target} under decision {decision}. The action is recorded in the financial ledger and cannot execute outside this context.
disbursements.previewText: Confirm the source, destination, and decision before recording the disbursement. Execution changes only this path balance.
disbursements.previewReady: Preview complete: this disbursement will debit only the selected path and link to this decision and spending item.
disbursements.previewNeedsSelection: Select a spending item, decision, and amount to see the financial impact.
disbursements.previewInsufficientBalance: Cannot execute: the amount is greater than the current path balance.
disbursements.previewSpendingCap: Cannot execute: the amount exceeds the spending item cap ({amount}).
disbursements.previewDecisionCap: Cannot execute: the amount exceeds the approved decision cap ({amount}).

disbursementRequests.title: Disbursement Requests
disbursementRequests.savedBeneficiaryOption: — Select a saved beneficiary or enter a new name —
disbursementRequests.externalBeneficiaryWarning: External beneficiary — name visible to committee only
disbursementRequests.linkDecisionOption: — Link disbursement decision (for approval and execution) —
disbursementRequests.decisionRequiredHint: Approval here requires selecting a closed and approved disbursement decision for the same path, spending item, and amount.
disbursementRequests.decisionRequiredForApproval: Select an approved disbursement decision before approving this request.
disbursementRequests.noApprovedDisbursementDecisions: No closed approved disbursement decisions exist for this path. Create and close a disbursement decision with approval first.
disbursementRequests.contextText: A disbursement request starts as a review request inside one path. It does not become an actual disbursement unless linked to a closed approved disbursement decision for the same path, item, and amount.
disbursementRequests.contextReviewerOutcome: You review requests here; final approval must be linked to a valid disbursement decision, and execution changes only this path balance.
disbursementRequests.contextRequesterOutcome: You can track your request here; approval or rejection should show a clear reason and next step.
disbursementRequests.requestLinkedDecision: This request is linked to a governed disbursement decision; execution must remain within the same path and amount.
disbursementRequests.requestAwaitingDecision: This request awaits review and a valid disbursement decision before final approval.
disbursementRequests.requestRejectedOutcome: The request was rejected; review notes should explain the reason and next step.
disbursementRequests.requestNoDecision: No linked disbursement decision is visible for this request.
disbursementRequests.rejectionReasonFallback: No clear rejection reason was recorded. Ask the administration for clarification before submitting again.
disbursementRequests.rejectionNextStep: If the reason is incomplete evidence, attach the missing documents and submit a new request. If you believe the rejection is unfair, open a dispute linked to this request.
disbursementRequests.ruleSummaryText: Please ensure the request complies with the allowed financial limits and that the purpose aligns with entity policies before approving.
disbursementRequests.cancelConfirmPrompt: Are you sure you want to cancel?

committees.title: Committees
committees.descriptionPlaceholder: Brief description of the committee's responsibilities
committees.chooseEntity: Select an entity to view its committees.
committees.myScopeTitle: These are the committees and paths tied to your role
committees.myScopeDescription: Entity committees are visible for transparency, but committee actions, voting, and review should only apply to paths where you are a committee member.
committees.myScopeEmpty: No committee is linked to your current membership. If you expected committee access, contact an entity admin.
committees.myScopeNote: If a decision or disbursement outside these paths appears as actionable, treat it as an excessive-permission issue.
committees.noDescription: No description is recorded for this committee.

beneficiaries.title: Beneficiaries
beneficiaries.empty: No registered beneficiaries in this entity.
beneficiaries.chooseEntity: Select an entity to view its beneficiaries.

platformAccess.title: Platform Team Access to Your Entity Data
platformAccess.desc1: All access by the CollectiveTrustOS team to your entity data is automatically logged here.
platformAccess.desc2: This log cannot be deleted or modified.
platformAccess.empty: No platform team members have accessed your entity data yet.

reviewCenter.emptyRecords: ✓ No payment proofs pending review — all receipts have been processed
reviewCenter.ruleRecordsTitle: Why are you reviewing these receipts?
reviewCenter.ruleRecordsDesc: You are the treasurer or entity admin. Your task is to verify that the receipt matches the required amount and confirm payment validity before approving.
reviewCenter.emptyDisbursements: ✓ No disbursement requests pending review at this time
reviewCenter.ruleDisbursementsTitle: Why are you asked to approve this disbursement?
reviewCenter.ruleDisbursementsDesc: Disbursement requests go through admin review before execution. Your approval triggers the next step in the entity's approved governance path.
reviewCenter.ruleDisputesTitle: Why are you reviewing these disputes?
reviewCenter.ruleDisputesDesc: The entity's dispute mechanism allows you as admin to settle or escalate disputes. Your decision is governed by the entity's internal policy.
reviewCenter.unauthorizedAdmin: This page is for founders and admins only
